tools.name: schema_parser
tools.description: Parse an OpenAPI 3.x schema object or GraphQL SDL string. Returns a structured list of endpoints (and GraphQL types).

tools.name: playground_renderer
tools.description: Render an interactive HTML API playground for a list of endpoints. Returns self-contained HTML with try-it-out forms.

tools.name: api_executor
tools.description: Execute a live HTTP request to an external URL. Returns real status, headers, and response body. Private IPs are blocked.

tools.name: code_generator
tools.description: Generate a ready-to-run code snippet for an HTTP request in javascript, typescript, python, curl, go, ruby, php, or java.

tools.name: history_tracker
tools.description: Get invocation history for a tool within the current Worker instance. Resets on cold start.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: playground_renderer · Private data: api_executor · Public sink: api_executor, code_generator · Destructive: api_executor, code_generator.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: playground_renderer · Private data: api_executor · Public sink: api_executor, code_generator · Destructive: api_executor, code_generator

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
